task: Go through ALL locale files (en, ru, zh, ar) — complete audit and improvement pass. Every string must be: (1) actually translated into the target language (no English left in zh/ar/ru), (2) natural-sounding native copy not machine-translated, (3) copy-optimized for a premium Dubai real estate brand. Fix grammatical issues, remove borrowed words where native alternatives exist, ensure the ListYourProperty feature bullets and all sections are fully translated. Also: remove all em dashes (—) from ALL locale files (en, ru, zh, ar) — they look AI-generated. Replace with commas, colons, periods, or rewrite the sentence naturally.

stages.spec:
## Why this will be good
Four locale files pushed to genuinely native copy for a premium Dubai real estate brand. No more AI-tell em dashes. Chinese buyers finally see real Mandarin, not English strings with Chinese chrome. Arabic reads like a native copywriter wrote it. Russian keyboard shortcuts stop looking like typographic poetry. The site will feel localized, not translated.

## Scope (content-only, no code)
Edit exactly 4 files: messages/en.json, messages/ru.json, messages/zh.json, messages/ar.json. Values only, never touch key names, nesting, or structure.

## Per-file work

### messages/zh.json — translate ~94 untranslated English values into natural Mandarin
- Translate every value that is still English prose into fluent, premium-brand Mandarin suitable for mainland Chinese investors and buyers eyeing Dubai real estate.
- Required native labels (from planner): 电子邮件 (Email), 电话 (Phone), 姓名 (Name), 留言 (Message). Apply these everywhere those field labels appear.
- Stay as-is (do not translate):
  - Brand names: Binayah Properties, WhatsApp, RERA, ROI, AI, PDF, PNG, JPG, WEBP, GIF
  - Email addresses: info@binayah.com, you@example.com, and any other literal email address
  - Copyright line (the © Binayah Properties ... line) stays in its current form
  - Language switcher labels (the list of language names) stay as-is
  - Numerals, currency symbols (AED, USD), URLs
- Tone: professional, aspirational, warm. Avoid stiff calque from English. Prefer concise idiomatic Mandarin phrasing over word-for-word translation. Use 您 (polite you) consistently for direct-address copy.
- ListYourProperty feature bullets and all section headings/CTAs must be fully translated. This was specifically flagged.

### messages/ar.json — quality pass + em-dash removal
- ar is effectively fully translated already. Focus is (a) remove every em dash, (b) smooth any awkward machine-translated phrasing, (c) keep the proper-noun / placeholder English values as-is (emails, brand names).
- Natural Arabic copy for the same premium brand tone. Prefer native Arabic terms over borrowed English where an idiomatic Arabic equivalent exists and reads well. No invented terminology: if the existing Arabic term is already natural, leave it.
- Em-dash replacement rules apply (see below). RTL rendering does not change the replacement logic.

### messages/en.json — remove all em dashes + light polish
- Remove every em dash (—). No exceptions.
- Replacement rules:
  - Comma when joining two related clauses of similar weight
  - Colon when introducing a detail, list, or explanation
  - Period (split into two sentences) when both halves stand alone as complete thoughts
  - Natural rewrite when none of the above produce clean copy
- Fix any awkward phrasing you notice during the pass, but do not rewrite values that already read well.

### messages/ru.json — remove all em dashes + quality check
- Remove every em dash (—) without exception. This includes keyboard-shortcut labels (e.g. 'Enter — поиск' becomes 'Enter: поиск' or 'Enter / поиск'). Pick whichever reads cleaner in context and be consistent within a single cluster of shortcut labels.
- Same clause-level replacement rules as en.json.
- Note: Russian copy legitimately uses em dashes as a subject-predicate connector in formal writing, but the user's directive is explicit and absolute. Zero em dashes in any locale file. Rewrite sentence structure where needed to preserve naturalness without the dash.

## Hard constraints
- Keys and nesting are untouched. Values only.
- All 4 files must parse as valid JSON when done. Verify with python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('messages/XX.json'))" for each.
- Key parity: every key in en.json must exist in zh.json and ar.json (ru.json too). No missing keys, no new keys.
- Zero em dashes (the U+2014 character —) anywhere in any of the 4 files after the pass. Verify with grep.
- Proper nouns, email addresses, brand names, language-switcher labels, and the copyright line remain in their original form across all locales.
- Do not edit any other file. Not a single .tsx, .ts, .css, or config file. Content-only task.

## Acceptance criteria (DEV self-check before committing)
1. grep -c '—' on each of the four files returns 0.
2. All 4 files parse as valid JSON.
3. zh.json has no substantive English prose values remaining (proper nouns, emails, brand names, language-switcher labels excepted).
4. ar.json has no regressions: the proper-noun English values that were intentionally English before remain English; no new English was introduced.
5. Key count parity: the sorted list of paths in en.json matches the sorted list of paths in zh/ar/ru.
6. Spot-check: the four Chinese UI labels 电子邮件 / 电话 / 姓名 / 留言 appear in zh.json where Email/Phone/Name/Message labels previously lived.

## Out of scope — do not do any of this
- Adding, removing, or renaming keys
- Changing nesting or file structure
- Any code, component, route, middleware, or config changes
- Adding new locale files
- Touching PROJECT_CONTEXT.md or any documentation

## DEV workflow hint
Work file by file. For zh.json, load the current file, identify every value that is still English prose (by eye or by a quick regex for ASCII-heavy values), and translate in place. Keep JSON structure identical. Validate JSON after each file before moving on. Commit once all four files pass validation and the grep returns zero em dashes across the board.

### messages/ru.json — additional: "Строящиеся" → "Новостройки"
- Throughout ru.json, every occurrence of "Строящиеся", "Строящееся", "Строящаяся" that refers to the off-plan/new-development concept must be changed to "Новостройки" (industry-standard Russian term for new builds / off-plan developments).
- Key spots to change (but scan the full file — do not miss any):
  - nav.offPlan: "Строящиеся" → "Новостройки"
  - search.tabOffPlan: "Строящееся" → "Новостройки"
  - search.offPlanProjects: "Строящиеся проекты" → "Новостройки"
  - home.offPlan (section title/label): anything mentioning "Строящиеся проекты" → "Новостройки"
  - savedProperties.offPlanBadge: "Строящееся" → "Новостройки"
  - Any page titles, breadcrumb labels, or meta descriptions that say "Строящиеся"
- Note: "Новостройки" is invariant (plural nominative) in context; it does not decline when used as a label/heading. For inline text that requires a different case, use the appropriate declined form ("новостроек", "новостройкам", etc.) — but most occurrences are headings/nav labels and should be "Новостройки".
